questions.title: Reverse String
questions.description: Write a function that reverses a string. The input string is given as an array of characters `s`. You must do this by modifying the input array in-place with O(1) extra memory.

starter.python:
def reverse_string(s):
    """
    Do not return anything, modify s in-place instead.
    """
    pass

hints: The entire logic for reversing a string is based on using the opposite directional two-pointer approach.
Swap the first and last elements, then move pointers inward.
questions.solution: Use two pointers, one at the start and one at the end. Swap the characters at these pointers and move them towards each other until they meet.

questions.title: Valid Parentheses
questions.description: Given a string `s` containing just the characters '(', ')', '{', '}', '[' and ']', determine if the input string is valid. An input string is valid if: Open brackets are closed by the same type of brackets, and open brackets are closed in the correct order.
hints: When you encounter an opening bracket, push it to the stack.
When you encounter a closing bracket, check if the top of the stack matches.
questions.solution: Push opening brackets onto a stack. When a closing bracket appears, pop the stack and ensure it matches the corresponding opening type. If the stack is empty at the end, the string is valid.

questions.title: Contains Duplicate
questions.description: Given an integer array `nums`, return `true` if any value appears at least twice in the array, and return `false` if every element is distinct.
hints: A Hash Set is a great way to check for duplicates.
Alternatively, you could sort the array first.
questions.solution: Iterate through the array and add each number to a Set. If a number is already in the Set, return true. If the loop finishes, return false.

questions.title: Maximum Subarray
questions.description: Given an integer array `nums`, find the subarray with the largest sum, and return its sum.
hints: Try to solve this using Kadane's Algorithm.
If the current sum becomes negative, it's better to start fresh from the next element.
questions.solution: Maintain a 'current_sum' and 'max_sum'. Iterate through the array, adding the current element to 'current_sum'. If 'current_sum' becomes less than the current element itself, reset 'current_sum' to that element. Update 'max_sum' at each step.

questions.title: Valid Anagram
questions.description: Given two strings `s` and `t`, return `true` if `t` is an anagram of `s`, and `false` otherwise.
hints: An anagram must have the same length.
Count the frequency of each character in both strings.
questions.solution: First, check if lengths are equal. Use a frequency map (or an array of size 26 for lowercase English) to count characters in `s` and decrement for `t`. If all counts are zero, it's an anagram.
questions.spaceComplexity: O(1) (since the alphabet size is constant)
